creator: jilles@FreeBSD.org
text:
Created attachment 187011
allow both current and POSIX-compliant sh -c

The COPYTREE_BIN and COPYTREE_SHARE macros use constructs like

sh -c 'command' -- arg0 arg1

and assume that $0 will expand to arg0 and $1 will expand to arg1.

Our current sh implements it that way. However, POSIX specifies that $0 will expand to -- and $1 will expand to arg0, since the command string is an operand and not an option-argument. Bug #220587 requests making sh POSIX-compliant in this regard.

If the argument after the command string does not start with '-', the current sh behaves as required by POSIX. Therefore, the macros should be adjusted so this is always the case.

Tested with poudriere, stable/10 with patched head sh, port textproc/docbook-sgml.
